info.name: Oracle Fusion – All Endpoints (JSON)
info.description:
Complete Oracle Fusion Cloud REST API collection with **JSON request/response bodies only**.

## Modules covered
| Folder | Module | Endpoints |
|--------|--------|-----------|
| **AR – Receivables** | Accounts Receivable | GET Customer Profile, POST Invoice, POST Standard Receipt, POST Apply Receipt, POST Miscellaneous Receipt |
| **SCM – Inventory** | Supply Chain Management | GET Items, GET On-Hand Qty, POST Available Qty, GET Orgs, GET Subinventories, GET UOM, GET Inv Transactions, POST Inv Transaction |
| **GL – General Ledger** | General Ledger | POST Import Journal |
| **Payments – Bank Accounts** | Payments | GET External Bank Accounts |

## Authentication
All requests use **HTTP Basic Authentication**. Set the following in the active Postman environment:
- `fusion_username` – Oracle Fusion Cloud username
- `fusion_password` – Oracle Fusion Cloud password
- `fusion_base_url` – Full base URL (e.g. `https://ehxk-test.fa.em2.oraclecloud.com`)

## AR Flow (complete POS sale sync)
Run the AR requests in this order:
1. **GET Customer Profile** – obtain `CustomerAccountId` → set `{{customer_account_id}}` variable
2. **POST Create Invoice** – obtain `TransactionNumber` → set `{{transaction_number}}` variable
3. **POST Create Standard Receipt** – obtain `ReceiptId` → set `{{receipt_id}}` variable
4. **POST Apply Receipt to Invoice** – clears the AR balance
5. **POST Create Miscellaneous Receipt** – only for cash-rounding lines
6. **POST Import Journal** – final GL posting step

## How to import
1. In Postman, click **Import** and select this file.
2. Import `Fusion_Environment.json` and set it as the active environment.
3. Fill in your Oracle Fusion Cloud credentials and instance URL.
4. Execute requests in the order shown above.

item.name: AR – Receivables
item.description:
Oracle Fusion Accounts Receivable (AR) REST endpoints.

**Recommended execution order for a complete POS sale sync:**
1. GET Customer Profile → note `CustomerAccountId` and `PaymentTerms`
2. POST Create Invoice → note `TransactionNumber`
3. POST Create Standard Receipt → note `ReceiptId` and set `{{receipt_id}}`
4. POST Apply Receipt to Invoice → links the receipt to the invoice
5. POST Create Miscellaneous Receipt → for cash-rounding adjustments (if applicable)

All endpoints use **HTTP Basic Authentication** (set `fusion_username` and `fusion_password` in the active environment).

request.description:
Retrieves an Oracle Fusion AR customer account record filtered by account number. The response `CustomerAccountId` is used as `customerId` in the Standard Receipt payload, and `PaymentTerms` is used as `PaymentTermsName` in the Invoice payload.

**Mapped to:** `FusionCustomerProfileClient.getCustomerAccountId()` / `getPaymentTerms()`
**REST path:** `GET /fscmRestApi/resources/11.13.17.11/customers`

### Query parameters
| Parameter | Example | Description |
|-----------|---------|-------------|
| q | CustomerNumber='ACC-001' | Filter expression |
| fields | CustomerAccountId,… | Comma-separated fields to include |
| limit | 1 | Max records to return |

body.raw:
{
  "BillToCustomerName": "Ahmed Al Qurashi Stores",
  "BillToAccountNumber": "ACC-001",
  "BillToLocation": "AE-DUBAI",
  "BusinessUnit": "Ibrahim AL Qurashi - KSA",
  "TransactionSource": "Manual",
  "TransactionType": "PASA CONSULTING SALE",
  "TransactionDate": "2024-03-14",
  "GlDate": "2024-03-14",
  "InvoiceCurrencyCode": "AED",
  "ConversionRateType": "Corporate",
  "PaymentTermsName": "Immediate",
  "invoiceLine": [
    {
      "LineNumber": 1,
      "ItemNumber": "OIL00001",
      "Description": "Pure Olive Oil 500ml \u2013 FLAMINGO outlet POS sale",
      "Quantity": 2,
      "UomCode": "Ea",
      "UnitSellingPrice": 250.0,
      "CurrencyCode": "AED",
      "SalesOrder": "INV-10001",
      "SalesOrderLine": "1",
      "TaxClassificationCode": "UAE-VAT-5"
    },
    {
      "LineNumber": 2,
      "MemoLineName": "DISCOUNT",
      "Description": "Promotional discount",
      "Quantity": 1,
      "UomCode": "Ea",
      "UnitSellingPrice": -25.0,
      "CurrencyCode": "AED",
      "SalesOrder": "INV-10001",
      "SalesOrderLine": "2"
    }
  ]
}

request.description:
Creates a Receivables invoice in Oracle Fusion AR for a VendHQ POS sale.

**Mapped to:** `FusionInvoiceClient.saveInvoice()` → `InvoiceService.createSimpleInvoice()`
**REST path:** `POST /fscmRestApi/resources/11.13.17.11/receivablesInvoices`

### InvoiceHeader fields
| Field | Type | Required | Example | Notes |
|-------|------|----------|---------|-------|
| BillToCustomerName | String | Yes | Ahmed Al Qurashi Stores | AR customer name |
| BillToAccountNumber | String | Yes | ACC-001 | AR customer account number |
| BillToLocation | String | Yes | AE-DUBAI | Customer site code |
| BusinessUnit | String | Yes | Ibrahim AL Qurashi - KSA | Fusion BU name |
| TransactionSource | String | Yes | Manual | Source of the transaction |
| TransactionType | String | Yes | PASA CONSULTING SALE | AR transaction type |
| TransactionDate | String | Yes | 2024-03-14 | Invoice date (YYYY-MM-DD) |
| GlDate | String | Yes | 2024-03-14 | GL accounting date (YYYY-MM-DD) |
| InvoiceCurrencyCode | String | Yes | AED | ISO 4217 currency code |
| ConversionRateType | String | Yes | Corporate | Rate type for conversion |
| PaymentTermsName | String | Yes | Immediate | Fetched from customer profile |

### invoiceLine fields
| Field | Type | Required | Notes |
|-------|------|----------|-------|
| LineNumber | Integer | Yes | Sequential line number |
| ItemNumber | String | No | Fusion item number (product lines) |
| MemoLineName | String | No | Used for discount/memo lines |
| Description | String | No | Line description |
| Quantity | Number | Yes | Quantity |
| UomCode | String | Yes | Unit of measure code e.g. Ea |
| UnitSellingPrice | Number | Yes | Negative for discounts |
| CurrencyCode | String | Yes | ISO 4217 |
| SalesOrder | String | No | VendHQ invoice number |
| SalesOrderLine | String | No | Line number string |
| TaxClassificationCode | String | No | Tax code e.g. UAE-VAT-5 |

Creates a cash or bank standard receipt in Oracle Fusion AR and links it to a payment method. Used for regular customer payments (Visa, Cash, Master Card, etc.).

**Mapped to:** `FusionReceiptClient.saveStandardReceipt()` → `StandardReceiptService.createStandardReceipt()`
**REST path:** `POST /fscmRestApi/resources/11.13.17.11/receivablesReceipts`

**Flow:** Call AFTER `POST Create Invoice`. The `ReceiptNumber` follows the pattern `{PaymentType}-{VendHQTransactionNumber}`.

### Request fields
| Field | Type | Required | Example | Notes |
|-------|------|----------|---------|-------|
| CurrencyCode | String | Yes | AED | ISO 4217 currency code |
| ReceiptDate | String | Yes | 2024-03-14 | Receipt date (YYYY-MM-DD) |
| GlDate | String | Yes | 2024-03-14 | GL date (YYYY-MM-DD) |
| DepositDate | String | Yes | 2024-03-14 | Bank deposit date |
| ReceiptMethodId | Long | Yes | 300000001001001 | Payment method ID from FusionReceiptMethod |
| ReceiptNumber | String | Yes | Cash-INV-10001 | Format: {PaymentType}-{TransactionNumber} |
| RemittanceBankAccountId | Long | Yes | 300000001100999 | From ExternalBankAccount lookup |
| CustomerId | Long | Yes | 300000001100001 | From Customer Profile lookup |
| OrgId | Long | Yes | 300000001001000 | Fusion business unit / org ID |
| Amount.Value | Number | Yes | 550.00 | Total receipt amount |
| Amount.CurrencyCode | String | Yes | AED | Must match CurrencyCode |

Applies an existing standard receipt to a specific customer invoice in Oracle Fusion AR, clearing the open receivable balance on that invoice.

**Mapped to:** `FusionReceiptClient.saveApplyStandardReceipt()` → `StandardReceiptService.createApplyReceipt()`
**REST path:** `POST /fscmRestApi/resources/11.13.17.11/receivablesReceipts/{ReceiptId}/child/applyReceiptOnAccount`

**Flow:** Run this AFTER both `POST Create Invoice` AND `POST Create Standard Receipt` have succeeded. Set `receipt_id` (Postman variable) to the `ReceiptId` returned by the Standard Receipt call.

### Request fields
| Field | Type | Required | Example | Notes |
|-------|------|----------|---------|-------|
| TransactionNumber | String | Yes | TXN-20240314-001 | Fusion invoice transaction number |
| ReceiptNumber | String | Yes | Cash-INV-10001 | Receipt number from previous step |
| AmountApplied | Number | Yes | 550.00 | Amount to apply |
| ReceiptCurrencyCode | String | Yes | AED | ISO 4217 |
| TransactionSource | String | Yes | Manual | Same source used on the invoice |
| ApplicationDate | String | Yes | 2024-03-14 | Application date |
| AccountingDate | String | Yes | 2024-03-14 | GL accounting date |

Creates a miscellaneous (non-customer) receipt in Oracle Fusion AR. Used for cash-rounding adjustments — any payment posted directly to a receivable activity account rather than applied to a customer invoice.

**Mapped to:** `FusionReceiptClient.saveMiscReceipt()` → `MiscellaneousReceiptService.createMiscellaneousReceipt()`
**REST path:** `POST /fscmRestApi/resources/11.13.17.11/receivablesMiscellaneousReceipts`

**Flow:** Run this AFTER `POST Create Invoice`. A negative `Amount.Value` represents a cash-rounding deduction.

### Request fields
| Field | Type | Required | Example | Notes |
|-------|------|----------|---------|-------|
| CurrencyCode | String | Yes | AED | ISO 4217 currency code |
| ReceiptDate | String | Yes | 2024-03-14 | Receipt date (YYYY-MM-DD) |
| GlDate | String | Yes | 2024-03-14 | GL date (YYYY-MM-DD) |
| ReceiptMethodId | Long | Yes | 300000001001002 | Payment method ID |
| ReceiptMethodName | String | Yes | Cash Rounding | Payment method name |
| ReceiptNumber | String | Yes | Cash-INV-10001-MISC | Format: {PaymentType}-{TxnNumber}-MISC |
| RemittanceBankAccountName | String | Yes | Main Cash Account | Bank/cash account name |
| ReceivableActivityName | String | Yes | Cash Rounding | Receivable activity to post to |
| OrgId | Long | Yes | 300000001001000 | Fusion business unit / org ID |
| Amount.Value | Number | Yes | -0.50 | Negative for cash-rounding deduction |
| Amount.CurrencyCode | String | Yes | AED | Must match CurrencyCode |

item.name: SCM – Inventory
item.description:
Oracle Fusion Supply Chain Management (SCM) REST endpoints for inventory management.

**Base path:** `{{fusion_base_url}}/fscmRestApi/resources/11.13.17.11/`

All endpoints use **HTTP Basic Authentication** (set `fusion_username` and `fusion_password` in the active environment). Set `org_code`, `item_number`, `last_sync_date`, and `bu_name` as needed before executing requests.

query.description: Filter: items updated after last_sync_date, type = Finished Good, in the given org
Fields to include in the response

request.description:
Retrieves Oracle Fusion inventory items updated after a specified date, filtered by type and organization. Used by `FusionItemsService.getFusionItems()` during the items sync job to push new/updated products to VendHQ.

**Mapped to:** `FusionItemsService.getFusionItems(Date lastFetchedDate, String orgCode)`
**REST path:** `GET /fscmRestApi/resources/11.13.17.11/items`

### Key query parameters
| Parameter | Example | Description |
|-----------|---------|-------------|
| q | LastUpdateDate>'2024-03-01';UserItemTypeValue=Finished Good;OrganizationCode=VWH | Filter expression |
| fields | ItemId,ItemNumber,… | Fields to return |
| orderBy | LastUpdateDate | Sort field |
| limit | 500 | Max records per page |

Set `last_sync_date` (e.g. `2024-03-01`) and `org_code` (e.g. `VWH`) in the Postman environment.

response.name: 200 OK – Items list
response.body:
{
  "items": [
    {
      "ItemId": 300000001100015,
      "OrganizationCode": "VWH",
      "OrganizationId": 300000001001005,
      "ItemNumber": "OIL00001",
      "ItemDescription": "Pure Olive Oil 500ml",
      "MarketPrice": 45.0,
      "PrimaryUOMCode": "Ea",
      "PrimaryUOMValue": "Each",
      "UserItemTypeValue": "Finished Good",
      "InventoryItemStatusCode": "Active",
      "LongDescription": "Cold-pressed pure olive oil \u2013 500ml bottle",
      "OutputTaxClassificationCodeValue": "UAE-VAT-5",
      "LastUpdateDate": "2024-03-14T10:30:00.000+03:00",
      "CreationDate": "2023-01-01T00:00:00.000+03:00",
      "links": [
        {
          "rel": "self",
          "href": "{{fusion_base_url}}/fscmRestApi/resources/11.13.17.11/items/300000001100015"
        }
      ]
    }
  ],
  "count": 1,
  "hasMore": false,
  "limit": 500,
  "offset": 0
}

query.description: Filter by item number and organization code

request.description:
Retrieves on-hand inventory quantity details per subinventory for a specific item and organization. Used by `FusionOnHandQtyService.getOnHandQtyDetails()` to determine current stock levels before triggering inventory transactions.

**Mapped to:** `FusionOnHandQtyService.getOnHandQtyDetails(String itemNumber, String orgCode)`
**REST path:** `GET /fscmRestApi/resources/11.13.17.11/onhandQuantityDetails`

Set `item_number` (e.g. `OIL00001`) and `org_code` (e.g. `VWH`) in the Postman environment.

Queries the available-to-transact quantity for a specific item in a subinventory. Uses a POST request with a JSON body (Oracle Fusion's REST pattern for complex queries).

**Mapped to:** `FusionAvailableQtyService.getAvailableQty(AvailableQuantityRequest)`
**REST path:** `POST /fscmRestApi/resources/11.13.17.11/availableQuantityDetails`

### Request fields
| Field | Type | Required | Example | Notes |
|-------|------|----------|---------|-------|
| OrganizationCode | String | Yes | VWH | Inventory organization code |
| ItemNumber | String | Yes | OIL00001 | Fusion item number |
| Subinventory | String | No | FLAMINGO | Subinventory name (optional filter) |

query.description: Filter by business unit name to resolve OrgId and BU ID

request.description:
Retrieves Oracle Fusion inventory organizations optionally filtered by Business Unit name. Used by `FusionInvOrgService.getbuIdFromName()` to resolve the `ManagementBusinessUnitId` from a BU name — this ID is stored in `OutletsIntegrationConfig` and used in receipt payloads as `OrgId`.

**Mapped to:** `FusionInvOrgService.getbuIdFromName(String buName)`
**REST path:** `GET /fscmRestApi/resources/11.13.17.11/inventoryOrganizations`

Set `bu_name` (e.g. `Ibrahim AL Qurashi - KSA`) in the Postman environment.

response.name: 200 OK – Organizations list
response.body:
{
  "items": [
    {
      "OrganizationId": 300000001001005,
      "OrganizationCode": "VWH",
      "OrganizationName": "Ibrahim AL Qurashi - KSA",
      "ManagementBusinessUnitId": 300000001001000,
      "ManagementBusinessUnitName": "Ibrahim AL Qurashi - KSA",
      "LegalEntityId": 300000001000001,
      "LegalEntityName": "Ibrahim Al Qurashi For Trade",
      "Status": "Active",
      "InventoryFlag": true
    }
  ],
  "count": 1,
  "hasMore": false,
  "limit": 1000,
  "offset": 0
}

query.description: Filter by subinventory name (outlet name in VendHQ)

request.description:
Retrieves active subinventories filtered by name. Used by `FusionSubinventoryService.getOrganizationName()` and `getOrganizationCode()` to map a VendHQ outlet name (e.g. FLAMINGO) to its Fusion organization code and name.

**Mapped to:** `FusionSubinventoryService.getOrganizationName(String subinventoryName)`
**REST path:** `GET /fscmRestApi/resources/11.13.17.11/activeSubinventories`

Set `subinventory_name` (e.g. `FLAMINGO`) in the Postman environment.

response.name: 200 OK – Subinventories
response.body:
{
  "items": [
    {
      "SubinventoryId": 300000001002001,
      "SecondaryInventoryName": "FLAMINGO",
      "OrganizationId": 300000001001005,
      "OrganizationCode": "VWH",
      "OrganizationName": "Ibrahim AL Qurashi - KSA",
      "Description": "Flamingo Mall outlet subinventory",
      "Nettable": "Yes",
      "AllowReservations": "Yes",
      "QuantityTracked": "Yes"
    }
  ],
  "count": 1,
  "hasMore": false,
  "limit": 25,
  "offset": 0
}

query.description: Filter by UOM name (e.g. Each, Gram)

request.description:
Looks up the Oracle Fusion UOM code for a given unit-of-measure name. Used by `FusionUomService.getUomCode()` to resolve a human-readable UOM name (e.g. 'Each') to its short code (e.g. 'Ea') before building invoice line payloads.

**Mapped to:** `FusionUomService.getUomCode(String uomName)`
**REST path:** `GET /fscmRestApi/resources/11.13.17.11/unitsOfMeasure`

Set `uom_name` in the Postman environment, e.g. `Each` or `Gram`. Leave the `q` parameter empty (remove it) to list all UOM codes.

query.description: Filter by org code and date

request.description:
Retrieves posted inventory transactions for a given organization and date range. Used to verify that inventory adjustments from VendHQ sales have been successfully posted in Oracle Fusion SCM.

**REST path:** `GET /fscmRestApi/resources/11.13.17.11/inventoryTransactions`

Set `org_code` (e.g. `VWH`) and `last_sync_date` (e.g. `2024-03-01`) in the Postman environment.

response.name: 200 OK – Inventory transactions
response.body:
{
  "items": [
    {
      "UniqueTransactionId": 100001,
      "TransactionHeaderId": 200001,
      "OrganizationId": 300000001001005,
      "OrganizationName": "Ibrahim AL Qurashi - KSA",
      "Item": "OIL00001",
      "Subinventory": "FLAMINGO",
      "TransactionQuantity": -2.0,
      "TransactionUnitOfMeasure": "Gram",
      "TransactionType": "Miscellaneous issue",
      "TransactionDate": "2024-03-14T10:30:00.000+03:00",
      "TransactionSourceName": "Vend POS"
    }
  ],
  "count": 1,
  "hasMore": false,
  "limit": 50,
  "offset": 0
}

request.description:
Posts one or more inventory transaction lines to Oracle Fusion SCM to adjust on-hand quantities. Called by `FusionInvTxnService.insertInvTransactions()` after each VendHQ sale is processed. Negative `TransactionQuantity` = stock issue (sale); positive = receipt.

**Mapped to:** `FusionInvTxnService.insertInvTransactions(List<TransactionLine>)`
**REST path:** `POST /fscmRestApi/resources/11.13.17.11/inventoryTransactions`

### TransactionLine fields
| Field | Type | Required | Example | Notes |
|-------|------|----------|---------|-------|
| OrganizationName | String | Yes | Ibrahim AL Qurashi - KSA | Inventory org |
| Item | String | Yes | OIL00001 | Fusion item number |
| Subinventory | String | Yes | FLAMINGO | Sub-inventory code (VendHQ outlet name) |
| TransactionType | String | Yes | Miscellaneous issue | Fusion transaction type |
| TransactionUnitOfMeasure | String | Yes | Gram | UOM name |
| TransactionQuantity | Number | Yes | -3.0 | Negative = issue, positive = receipt |
| TransactionDate | String | Yes | 2024-03-14T10:30:00+03:00 | ISO-8601 with TZ offset |

response.name: 200 OK – Transactions created
response.body:
{
  "ReturnStatus": "S",
  "ReturnMessageText": "Inventory transactions created successfully.",
  "TransactionHeaderId": 987654321,
  "transactionLines": [
    {
      "UniqueTransactionId": 100002,
      "TransactionHeaderId": 987654321,
      "OrganizationName": "Ibrahim AL Qurashi - KSA",
      "Item": "OIL00001",
      "Subinventory": "FLAMINGO",
      "TransactionType": "Miscellaneous issue",
      "TransactionQuantity": -3.0,
      "TransactionDate": "2024-03-14T10:30:00+03:00"
    }
  ]
}

item.name: GL – General Ledger
item.description:
Oracle Fusion General Ledger (GL) REST endpoints.

**Base path:** `{{fusion_base_url}}/fscmRestApi/resources/11.13.17.11/`

The journal import is the final step in the VendHQ → Oracle Fusion AR integration flow, called after all AR invoices and receipts have been successfully created.

body.raw:
{

request.description:
Imports a General Ledger journal batch into Oracle Fusion GL. Called by `FusionJournalClient.saveImportJournal()` after all AR receipts for a VendHQ sale have been successfully created.

**Mapped to:** `FusionJournalClient.saveImportJournal(JournalHeader)`
**REST path:** `POST /fscmRestApi/resources/11.13.17.11/journals`

**Important:** A balanced journal entry requires at least one debit line and one credit line whose amounts sum to zero. The example below shows one credit (Revenue) and one debit (AR).

### JournalHeader fields
| Field | Type | Required | Example | Notes |
|-------|------|----------|---------|-------|
| BatchName | String | Yes | Mar-24: VENDHQ-INV-10001 | Unique batch identifier |
| BatchDescription | String | No | Journal Import for... | Description |
| LedgerId | Long | Yes | 300000001418025 | Fusion GL ledger ID |
| AccountingPeriodName | String | Yes | Mar-24 | GL period in format Mon-YY |
| AccountingDate | String | Yes | 2024-03-14 | Accounting date (YYYY-MM-DD) |
| UserSourceName | String | Yes | Vend | Journal source (must exist in GL setup) |
| UserCategoryName | String | Yes | Vend | Journal category (must exist in GL setup) |
| ErrorToSuspenseFlag | Boolean | No | false | Post errors to suspense account |
| SummaryFlag | Boolean | No | false | Summary vs detail posting |

### JournalLine fields (per line)
| Field | Type | Required | Notes |
|-------|------|----------|-------|
| LedgerId | Long | Yes | Same as header |
| PeriodName | String | Yes | Same as header AccountingPeriodName |
| AccountingDate | String | Yes | YYYY-MM-DD |
| JeSourceName | String | Yes | Same as UserSourceName |
| JeCategoryName | String | Yes | Same as UserCategoryName |
| GroupId | Long | Yes | VendHQ invoice number (as Long) |
| ChartOfAccountsId | Long | Yes | Chart of accounts ID |
| Segment1–Segment10 | String | Yes | 10-segment chart-of-accounts values |
| CurrencyCode | String | Yes | ISO 4217 |
| EnteredCrAmount / EnteredDrAmount | Number | Yes (one) | Credit or debit (not both) |
| AccountedCr / AccountedDr | Number | Yes (one) | Accounted amount |
| CurrencyConversionType | String | Yes | Corporate or User |
| CurrencyConversionDate | String | Yes | YYYY-MM-DD |
| CurrencyConversionRate | Number | Yes | 1 if same currency |
| Status | String | No | P = Posted |
| TaxCode | String | No | N = No tax |
| TransactionDate | String | Yes | YYYY-MM-DD |

item.name: Payments – Bank Accounts
item.description:
Oracle Fusion Payments REST endpoints for external bank account lookup.

**Base path:** `{{fusion_base_url}}/fscmRestApi/resources/11.13.17.11/`

The `ExternalBankAccountId` retrieved here is passed as `RemittanceBankAccountId` in Standard Receipt requests and as `RemittanceBankAccountName` in Miscellaneous Receipt requests.

request.description:
Retrieves external bank accounts filtered by account number. The returned `ExternalBankAccountId` is stored in `FusionReceiptMethod` and used as `RemittanceBankAccountId` in the Standard Receipt and `RemittanceBankAccountName` in the Miscellaneous Receipt payloads.

**Mapped to:** `FusionExternalBankClient.getBankAccountId(params, credential, accountNumber)`
**REST path:** `GET /fscmRestApi/resources/11.13.17.11/cashBankAccounts`

Set `bank_account_number` in the Postman environment (e.g. `1011203`). Remove the `q` parameter to list all bank accounts.

response.name: 200 OK – Bank accounts found
response.body:
{
  "items": [
    {
      "ExternalBankAccountId": 300000001100999,
      "BankAccountNumber": "1011203",
      "BankAccountName": "Main Cash Account \u2013 KSA",
      "CurrencyCode": "AED",
      "BankName": "Al Rajhi Bank",
      "BranchName": "Riyadh Main Branch",
      "IBAN": "SA0000000000000000000001",
      "AccountType": "Savings",
      "Status": "Active"
    }
  ],
  "count": 1,
  "hasMore": false,
  "limit": 25,
  "offset": 0
}
